items.title: Tanh
items.description: Tanh is the zero-centered cousin of sigmoid: output (−1, 1) with σ_tanh(x) = 2σ(2x)−1. Tanh shares saturation and vanishing-gradient issues but avoids the always-positive activation bias of sigmoid. Compare when choosing hidden-layer squashing in shallow nets or RNNs.

items.title: ReLU & Variants
items.description: ReLU (max(0,x)) became the default hidden activation because its gradient is 1 for positive inputs — no 0.25 cap per layer. When designing deep feedforward networks, prefer ReLU family over stacked sigmoid hidden units to avoid vanishing gradients.

items.title: Logistic Regression
items.description: Classical binary classification applies σ to a linear score ŷ = σ(wᵀx+b). Logistic Regression owns the sigmoid + BCE training workflow in supervised ML; this topic owns the activation function itself — formula, range, saturation, and deep-net gradient behavior.

items.title: Cross-Entropy
items.description: Binary cross-entropy is the standard loss when a sigmoid output represents P(y=1|x). Cross-Entropy defines the loss formulas; frameworks often fuse sigmoid+BCE as BCEWithLogitsLoss for numerical stability while still using σ at inference.
